units.description: New skills/development-harness/scripts/dispatch_batch.py: creates git worktrees under .harness/worktrees/<batch_id>/<unit_id>, creates harness/<batch_id>/<unit_id> branches, seeds WORKTREE_UNIT.json, writes state.execution.fleet; atomic teardown on failure
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/scripts/dispatch_batch.py: new 207-line stdlib-only module. Public API: dispatch_batch(batch_result, root, state=None, now=None) takes the {batch_id, batch, excluded} dict from compute_parallel_batch and per unit runs 'git worktree add -b harness/<batch_id>/<unit_id> .harness/worktrees/<batch_id>/<unit_id> HEAD' (via subprocess), seeds '<worktree>/.harness/WORKTREE_UNIT.json' with batch_id/unit_id/phase_id/touches_paths, and returns the updated fleet block. When a 'state' dict is passed, state.execution.fleet is replaced in place with {mode='dispatched', batch_id, units=[...]} where every entry matches the v2 state schema (status='running', started_at=now, ended_at=null, agent_summary_path=null, conflict=null). Atomic failure semantics: any subprocess.CalledProcessError, OSError, or KeyError raised during per-unit processing triggers _rollback(root, created) which best-effort 'git worktree remove --force' + 'git branch -D' every worktree/branch the call created before re-raising as DispatchError. CLI: --batch <file> --root <dir> --state <file>

skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/test_dispatch_batch.py: new 194-line test module with 9 cases across 3 classes. TestHappyPath: creates worktrees + branches + WORKTREE_UNIT.json contents + fleet-entry shape + empty-batch edge + state-is-written-in-place. TestAtomicRollback: pre-creates a colliding branch on unit_b so the second 'git worktree add' fails and asserts unit_a's worktree + branch are removed after DispatchError (pre-existing unit_b branch untouched); second case exercises the KeyError path by omitting 'id' on the second unit and asserts same rollback shape. TestCliSmoke: end-to-end via subprocess asserting stdout JSON, state.json mutation including last_updated, plus --help

Test fixtures use a real tempdir git repo (git init + user config + one commit) because worktree ops require a non-trivial commit graph. The helper _init_repo(path) is local to the test module; this is the first git-subprocess-using test file in the suite

Validation evidence with per-layer timing (per unit_018 rule): `python -m py_compile skills/development-harness/scripts/dispatch_batch.py skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/test_dispatch_batch.py` exits 0 (0.14s); `python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_dispatch_batch -v` passes 9/9 (3.5s); `python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests` passes 118/118 up from 109 (11.3s wall-clock)

units.description: New skills/development-harness/scripts/merge_batch.py: serial fan-in via git merge --no-ff; conflict_strategy=abort_batch or serialize_conflicted; post-merge validation; git reset --hard rollback on failure
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/scripts/merge_batch.py: new 217-line stdlib-only module. Public API: merge_batch(state, root, *, conflict_strategy='abort_batch', run_post_merge_validation=None, now=None). Flips fleet.mode to 'merging', captures pre_merge_ref = git rev-parse HEAD, iterates fleet.units in order running 'git merge --no-ff <branch> -m "harness: merge <unit_id>"'. On conflict: 'git merge --abort' always, then conflict_strategy decides -- abort_batch flips the unit's status to 'failed' with conflict={paths, strategy_applied} and flags remaining units as 'failed' (conflict=null, they were skipped); serialize_conflicted leaves the unit status='running' with conflict recorded so its worktree + branch stay alive for a later batch. Post-merge runs run_post_merge_validation(root, merged_unit_ids) (default: _noop_validate that always returns True) and on failure does 'git reset --hard <pre_merge_ref>' then downgrades every previously merged unit to 'failed' with strategy_applied='post_merge_validation_failed'. On success, every merged unit has its worktree ('git worktree remove --force') and branch ('git branch -D') cleaned up. Return value names the outcome: 'ok' | 'partial' | 'aborted' | 'validation_failed' | 'all_conflicted' | 'empty'. CLI: --state --root --conflict-strategy (choices validated at argparse level)
Scope-violation detection is left for unit_022 and .harness/.lock mutex for unit_023 -- both are clean insertion points in merge_batch's pre-per-unit and wrapper layers respectively

skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/test_merge_batch.py: new 340-line test module with 16 cases across 7 classes. TestHappyPath: 5 cases (all clean, commit messages, branches deleted, empty fleet, mode transitions). TestAbortBatchStrategy: 2 cases (abort semantics, branch-cleanup asymmetry -- merged branch deleted, conflicted + skipped branches preserved). TestSerializeConflictedStrategy: 2 cases (conflicted stays running + remaining merge, conflicted branch preserved). TestPostMergeValidation: 3 cases (validator called with correct merged_ids, reset --hard rollback on failure with HEAD verified at pre-merge SHA, validator not called when no unit merged). TestStrategyValidation: unknown strategy raises MergeError. TestEndToEndWithDispatch: full dispatch_batch -> commit in worktree -> merge_batch -> worktrees removed cycle. TestCliSmoke: --help and end-to-end state.json write

Validation evidence with per-layer timing: `python -m py_compile` on both new files exits 0 (0.13s); `python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_merge_batch` passes 16/16 (12.0s); `python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests` passes 134/134 up from 118 (21.6s)

units.description: New skills/development-harness/scripts/teardown_batch.py: idempotent cleanup of worktrees and harness/batch_* branches; safe to run under /clear, /sync, and error recovery
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/scripts/teardown_batch.py: new 146-line stdlib-only module. Public API: teardown_batch(root, batch_id=None) -> {removed_worktrees, deleted_branches, batch_ids}. Scoped mode (batch_id='<id>') cleans just that batch; global mode (batch_id=None) cleans every .harness/worktrees/batch_*/ directory and every 'harness/batch_*/' branch. Every git call runs with check=False so missing worktrees / missing branches / already-pruned entries never raise. Orphaned on-disk worktree directories that git no longer knows about are scrubbed via shutil.rmtree after the 'git worktree remove --force' best-effort. Empty batch dirs and the worktrees root are auto-pruned. Final 'git worktree prune' resyncs git's metadata. CLI: --batch-id (default all) --root

skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/test_teardown_batch.py: new 172-line test module with 10 cases across 4 classes. TestScopedTeardown (4): single-batch teardown removes worktrees + branches + dir, second run is a no-op, scoped teardown leaves other batches alone, phantom batch_id is a no-op. TestGlobalTeardown (2): global mode removes every batch + every harness/* branch, clean-repo global teardown is a no-op. TestMissingStateTolerance (2): orphaned branch without worktree still gets deleted and is reported; orphaned on-disk worktree dir without branch still gets scrubbed. TestCliSmoke (2): --help + end-to-end scoped CLI invocation

Idempotence property verified: TestScopedTeardown.test_second_run_is_a_no_op asserts the second call returns empty removed_worktrees + deleted_branches + batch_ids. TestGlobalTeardown.test_global_teardown_on_clean_repo_is_a_no_op asserts the same on a pristine repo with no dispatch history

Validation evidence with per-layer timing: `python -m py_compile` on both new files exits 0 (0.15s); `python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_teardown_batch -v` passes 10/10 (4.4s); `python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests` passes 144/144 up from 134 (27.8s)

units.description: Scope-violation detector in merge_batch.py: computes git diff --name-only <merge-base>..<branch> and rejects any unit whose diff exceeds declared touches_paths, regardless of sub-agent self-report
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/scripts/merge_batch.py: added three new helpers -- _is_within_scope(file_path, touches_paths) using fnmatch.fnmatchcase (fnmatch's `*` already matches path separators so `src/auth/**` naturally covers descendants); _scope_violations(root, branch, touches_paths) that runs `git merge-base HEAD <branch>` then `git diff --name-only <merge-base>..<branch>` and returns files matching no declared glob; _read_worktree_touches_paths(root, worktree_path) that reads the WORKTREE_UNIT.json sentinel seeded by dispatch_batch. Integration: the per-unit loop in merge_batch() now calls _scope_violations before _merge_unit. On violation, the unit is rejected with status='failed' and conflict={paths: <violators>, category: 'scope_violation'} -- NO merge is attempted and HEAD does not advance. Scope failures are always hard rejects regardless of conflict_strategy (don't conflate with merge conflicts). Other well-scoped units in the same batch still get their chance to merge
Also added `category` field to the existing merge-conflict conflict dict ('merge_conflict') for taxonomy consistency; `strategy_applied` is preserved so existing tests stay green

skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/test_scope_violation.py: new 276-line test module with 16 cases across 4 classes. TestIsWithinScope (6): literal match, recursive glob, non-recursive glob, non-match, empty scope rejects all, multi-glob any-match. TestScopeViolationsHelper (4): in-scope branch returns []; out-of-scope files returned verbatim; empty touches_paths flags every changed file; unknown branch yields []. TestReadWorktreeTouchesPaths (3): reads what dispatch seeded; missing sentinel returns None (skip check); empty list is preserved (not coerced to None). TestMergeBatchRejectsScopeViolator (3): scope violator rejected before merge with HEAD unchanged + branch preserved; well-scoped sibling merges alongside scope violator (scope failures don't trigger abort_batch); within-scope changes merge cleanly

Validation evidence with per-layer timing: `python -m py_compile` on the edited module + new test file exits 0 (0.14s); `python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_scope_violation -v` passes 16/16 (4.0s); `python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests` passes 160/160 up from 144 (27.8s) -- zero regressions in existing test_merge_batch.py despite merge_batch.py's internal changes

units.description: Add .harness/.lock file-based mutex around merge_batch.py with O_EXCL; contention test verifies serialization
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/scripts/merge_batch.py: added _MergeLock context manager backed by os.open(path, O_CREAT | O_EXCL | O_WRONLY) on <root>/.harness/.lock. First acquirer wins immediately; subsequent acquirers block, polling on lock_poll_interval (default 0.1s) until the file is unlinked (normal release) or its mtime exceeds stale_after_seconds (take-over after crashed holder). If lock_timeout (default 300s) elapses, MergeError is raised. Lock file body is '<pid> <iso_ts>\n' for human inspection and stale debugging. Release removes the file on both normal exit AND exception paths (via __exit__ always calling unlink, verified by test_release_removes_lock_file_even_on_exception). merge_batch() wraps its full flow in `with _MergeLock(root, timeout=..., stale_after_seconds=..., poll_interval=...)` -- factored the original body into _merge_batch_locked(). CLI gets new --lock-timeout and --lock-stale-after flags passing through to merge_batch. Defaults: DEFAULT_LOCK_TIMEOUT=300.0, DEFAULT_LOCK_STALE_AFTER=600.0, DEFAULT_LOCK_POLL_INTERVAL=0.1

skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/test_merge_batch.py: added TestLockContention with 4 cases. test_second_acquirer_blocks_until_first_releases: thread A acquires + holds for 0.4s; thread B blocks then acquires; assertion `b_time >= 0.3` verifies B waited. Event-barrier ensures A owns the lock before B tries, making the test robust against scheduling jitter. test_release_removes_lock_file_even_on_exception: raising RuntimeError inside `with _MergeLock(...)` still unlinks the lock file. test_stale_lock_is_taken_over: writes a lock file with mtime 10000s old, acquires with stale_after=300 in <0.5s. test_timeout_raises_merge_error_when_lock_is_fresh: pre-existing FRESH lock, lock_timeout=0.3s, merge_batch raises MergeError after >=0.25s wall-clock with 'timed out' in the error text; the pre-existing lock file is preserved (not stomped)

Backward compatibility: the lock wrapper is transparent to existing tests. test_merge_batch + test_scope_violation (32 cases) pass unchanged after the wrapper was introduced -- the fresh tempdir per test means no cross-test lock contention

Validation evidence with per-layer timing: `python -m py_compile skills/development-harness/scripts/merge_batch.py` exits 0 (0.14s); `python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_merge_batch.TestLockContention` passes 4/4 (1.6s); `python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests` passes 164/164 up from 160 (34.5s)

units.description: Extend skills/development-harness/scripts/sync_harness.py to detect orphaned worktrees, stale fleet entries, and harness/batch_* branches lacking fleet entries
validation_evidence: skills/development-harness/scripts/sync_harness.py: added _list_on_disk_worktrees (scans .harness/worktrees/<batch>/<unit>/ structure), _list_harness_branches (shells to `git branch --list --format=...`, filters for names matching `harness/batch_*/<unit>`, silently returns [] when git is unavailable -- sync is informational), and _detect_fleet_drift (cross-references state.execution.fleet.units against disk worktrees and git branches). run_sync now loads state.json (optional) and appends fleet-drift findings to the existing divergences array

Three new divergence types reported with actionable ids: orphan_worktree {type, worktree_path, batch_id, unit_id} for on-disk dirs not in fleet; stale_fleet_entry {type, unit_id, worktree_path, branch, batch_id} for fleet entries whose worktree_path is missing on disk; orphan_branch {type, branch, batch_id, unit_id} for harness/batch_*/<unit> branches without fleet entries. Each carries enough identity for downstream cleanup (feed batch_id to teardown_batch, unit_id to re-dispatch, etc.)

skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/test_sync_harness.py: added TestFleetDriftDetection (5 cases) with helpers _init_git_repo, _write_state_with_fleet, _minimal_phase_graph. Cases: on-disk worktree without fleet entry is orphan; fleet entry without on-disk worktree is stale; harness branch without fleet entry is orphan; clean state (worktree + fleet entry + matching branch) yields zero fleet-drift divergences; one of each divergence type in the same run is reported separately (by_type dict). Existing 3 TestSyncHarness tests pass unchanged

Validation evidence with per-layer timing: `python -m py_compile skills/development-harness/scripts/sync_harness.py` exits 0 (~0.1s); `python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_sync_harness -v` passes 8/8 (2.1s); `python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests` passes 169/169 up from 164 (35.3s)

units.description: Integration test skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/integration/test_parallel_invoke.py: fixture git repo with 3-unit phase, shell-scripted fake agents, asserts final state and cleanup

New skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/agents/harness-unit.md: system prompt, tool allowlist (no git push; no writes to .harness/; no writes outside worktree), required JSON report schema
Update skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/rules/harness-core.md with orchestrator-vs-sub-agent rules (only orchestrator merges; sub-agents commit only in worktree; never push/rebase)
Mirror orchestrator-vs-sub-agent rules in skills/development-harness/templates/rules/harness-core.mdc for Cursor

Rewrite skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md into a single batch-driven flow: batch of 1 runs in-tree, batch of N fans out via Agent(harness-unit); same dispatch/merge/validate/commit checkpoints
Mirror the single-flow rewrite in skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md
Document one-turn-per-batch semantics in skills/development-harness/references/architecture.md (session_count increments once per turn)
Wire parallel phase-review dispatch (code-review skill concurrent with commit-agent-changes) into the rewritten flow
End-to-end integration test: 3-unit fixture phase with parallelism.enabled=true completes with one merge; batch-of-1 test produces identical final state to a worktree batch of 1
Update skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py: pre-check that stops when state.execution.fleet.mode != 'idle'; deletes .invoke-active on stop
Mirror fleet-mode guard in skills/development-harness/templates/hooks/continue-loop.py (Cursor variant)
Unit tests for both hooks simulating fleet.mode = 'dispatched' (stop) and 'idle' (existing behavior); manual verification checklist for mid-batch kill
Session-scoped kill switch: write .harness/.parallel-disabled after 2 scope_violation or ambiguity failures in a session; cleared when .invoke-active clears
Verify batch-of-1 uses the same in-tree path as parallelism=false; re-assert via test that no special-case branch exists in invoke command docs
Document scope-violation always-on policy in skills/development-harness/references/phase-contract.md
Concurrent merge serialization test: two merge_batch.py invocations contend on .harness/.lock; second waits for first
Extend skills/development-harness/templates/checkpoint-template.md with a Batch section (batch ID, mode, per-unit status and branch, conflicts summary)
Update skills/development-harness/commands/state.md and templates/workspace-commands/harness-state.md to render fleet status, orphaned worktrees, and per-batch timings
Log directory creation logic for .harness/logs/<batch_id>/ with batch.json, <unit_id>.md, merge.log, validation.log; logs are harness-owned and gitignored
Integration test running a sample batch asserts all log artifacts are produced and /harness-state renders fleet + log pointers

ISSUE_001 fix: in skills/development-harness/commands/create.md Phase 5 (Hook configuration), apply the existing Python-detection pattern (PY=$(command -v python3 2>/dev/null || command -v python 2>/dev/null)) and bake the detected interpreter into the generated .claude/settings.local.json Stop-hook command ("$PY .claude/hooks/continue-loop.py"). Mirror the change for .cursor/hooks.json. Keep the hook script's shebang intact but no longer rely on it.

ISSUE_002 fix: retire the Stop-hook-as-continuation-driver for Claude Code (Cursor hook stays unchanged). (a) Add new doc skills/development-harness/references/claude-code-continuation.md explaining why Cursor's followup_message protocol cannot port to Claude Code's one-shot stop_hook_active guard. (b) Rewrite skills/development-harness/templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py to a precondition-only shape: run the authority chain (flag, budget, blockers, open_questions, selector-vs-checkpoint agreement), print an advisory to stdout describing next_unit or the reason to stop, always exit 0; do not attempt exit-2 block-continue. (c) Add 'Claude Code: run under /loop' sections to skills/development-harness/commands/invoke.md and skills/development-harness/templates/workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md pointing Claude Code users at '/loop /invoke-development-harness' as the autonomous-run primitive; document that direct /invoke-development-harness runs exactly one unit per turn on Claude Code by protocol. (d) Update skills/development-harness/commands/create.md Phase 5 (Hook configuration) so Claude Code installs wire the hook as a precondition checker (not a continue-driver) and the generated .claude/settings.local.json reflects the new role. After this lands, PHASE_008 (Stop-hook fleet-awareness, units 034-036) needs to be re-scoped so its fleet-mode checks layer onto the precondition-checker shape, not the retired block-continue shape.
acceptance_criteria: references/claude-code-continuation.md exists and names the one-shot stop_hook_active guard and Cursor's followup_message as the structural mismatch
templates/claude-code/hooks/continue-loop.py contains no sys.exit(2) and no {'decision': 'block'} emission; authority chain remains but output is advisory-only

commands/invoke.md and workspace-commands/invoke-development-harness.md both carry a 'Claude Code: run under /loop' section naming '/loop /invoke-development-harness' as the autonomous-run entry point
commands/create.md Phase 5 describes the Claude Code hook as a precondition checker and does not describe block-continue semantics for Claude Code
Cursor hook templates/hooks/continue-loop.py is unchanged (diff: 0 lines)
units.validation_method: docs-linter tests asserting each acceptance bullet; hook exit-code test for the rewritten Claude Code template (precondition-only shape)

units.description: Add 'Parallel Execution Model' section to skills/development-harness/references/architecture.md: worktree-per-unit, orchestrator/sub-agent boundary, frontier+overlap check, dispatch-wait-merge lifecycle, conflict strategies, when to enable/not

Update skills/development-harness/references/phase-contract.md to document required depends_on, touches_paths (when parallel_safe: true), and parallel_safe; add 'Decomposing a phase for parallelism' subsection
New skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md: dispatch lifecycle, overlap-matrix algorithm, merge-order rationale, failure-mode catalog, recovery procedures, readiness checklist

Update skills/development-harness/commands/create.md Phase 2 with two structured questions: (1) Enable parallel unit execution? (y/n, default n) -> config.execution_mode.parallelism.enabled; (2) Break-on-schema-bump vs migrate? (break/migrate, default break) -> config.execution_mode.versioning.break_on_schema_bump. Update Phase 4 to instruct proposing touches_paths per unit and setting parallel_safe: false when blast radius is unknown

Update skills/development-harness/SKILL.md with 'Parallel execution (optional)' subsection and 'Version upgrades' note: re-run /create-development-harness on schema_version changes; no migration script provided
Cross-check pass: every new field, script, rule, and command introduced by this roadmap is referenced in at least one doc under skills/development-harness/references/

Add parallelism readiness checklist to skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md: touches_paths on every parallelism-eligible unit, no shared-aggregator units, CI handles multi-commit pushes
Add 'Version upgrades' note to skills/development-harness/SKILL.md: re-run /create-development-harness when schema_version changes; no migration script is provided by design

Update skills/development-harness/commands/update.md (and the workspace template) to detect schema_version mismatch between installed harness and skill; emit a pointer to the recreate flow; test coverage via a v1 fixture

Create fixture at skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/fixtures/self-test/: throwaway workspace with a 2-phase / 6-unit mini-CRUD roadmap designed to exercise a batch >= 2, one merge conflict, and one scope violation
Run /create-development-harness on the fixture; populate touches_paths on every unit; enable parallelism; capture the generated phase-graph and config for the record
Run /invoke-development-harness iteratively until all fixture phases complete; ensure the run exercises batch >= 2, one merge conflict, and one scope violation
Capture execution traces; produce a data table comparing wall-clock with parallelism enabled vs a sequential baseline; verify no orphaned worktrees/branches after completion
Write post-mortem under skills/development-harness/scripts/tests/fixtures/self-test/POST-MORTEM.md and link it from skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md
